name: Varunprasaath R. Selvaraju, Ph.D.
summary: PhD Chemist with 6+ years of experience in analytical chemistry, chromatography, and mass spectrometry applications. Expertise in method development, materials characterization, and analytical problem-solving. Hands-on experience with LC-MS, GC-MS, FT-IR, UV-Vis, and thermal analysis techniques. Strong background in small molecule analysis, polymer characterization, and quality control. Passionate about developing innovative LC-MS solutions and supporting customers in achieving their analytical goals.

education.details: Dissertation: Modular Design and Electrochemical Properties of Xanthene-Based Electrochromic Materials

education.degree: B.S. (Research), Major: Chemistry; Minor: Materials Science

publications: Ramasamy Selvaraju, V. et al. 'Narrow-Band Electrochromism of a Ferrocene-Substituted Rhodamine B.' Chemistry of Materials, 2025, 37, 5558–5568.

bullets: Develop analytical methods for polymer characterization including chromatographic analysis
Apply statistical methods to optimize separation conditions and improve method performance
Developed analytical methods for small molecule characterization using multiple techniques including mass spectrometry
Conducted LC-MS and GC-MS analysis of organic compounds and degradation products
Validated analytical methods for quantitative analysis and impurity profiling
Applied spectroscopic and chromatographic techniques to solve complex analytical problems
Managed analytical instrumentation including LC-MS, GC-MS, FT-IR, and thermal analysis systems
Developed SOPs and trained researchers on proper chromatographic techniques and instrument operation
Implemented preventive maintenance programs ensuring reliable instrument performance

skills.Materials Characterization: FT-IR, UV-Vis-NIR, TGA, DSC, ellipsometry, cyclic voltammetry
skills.Process Development: DOE, SPC, statistical analysis, process optimization

projects.title: Bio-based Polymer Process Development
projects.description: Led chemical process development for novel bio-based polymer systems using statistical experimental design.

projects.title: AFOSR Defense Coatings Program
projects.description: Co-led process development for high-performance molecular thin-film coatings for defense optical applications.
